'''Derrell Olpherts''' (born 7 January 1992) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er, {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[Salford Red Devils]], the [[Castleford Tigers]] and the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[Hemel Stags]] and [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].

==Background==
Olpherts was born in [[Wakefield]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Career==
=== Dewsbury Rams ===
Olpherts made his professional début on 19 February 2012 for [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] in a cup tie against [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dewsburyreporter.co.uk/sport/spicer-is-big-loss-for-rams-1-4279498|title=Spicer is big loss for Rams|publisher=Dewsbury Reporter|date=24 February 2012|accessdate=22 March 2018}}</ref>

=== Hemel Stags ===
Olpherts played for the [[Hemel Stags]] in the [[RFL League 1|Kingstone Press League 1]] in 2015, making 22 appearances and scoring 7 tries as a fullback.<ref name="RKC" />

=== Newcastle Thunder ===
Olpherts represented the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]] between 2016 and 2017. He was utilised as a centre or fullback and scored 30 tries in 41 appearances.<ref name="RKC" />

=== Salford Red Devils ===
Olpherts joined the [[Salford Red Devils]] for the 2018 season.<ref name="BBC3Jul17">{{cite web |title=Derrell Olpherts: Newcastle Thunder back to join Salford Red Devils |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40484357 |website=BBC Sport |accessdate=3 July 2019 |date=3 July 2017}}</ref> He made his [[Super League]] début for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]].<ref>{{cite web |date=22 March 2018 |title=Salford Red Devils' Derrell Olpherts set to make debut |url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12207/11298990/salford-red-devils-derrell-olpherts-set-to-make-debut |publisher=Sky Sports |accessdate=22 March 2018}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
In July 2019, Olpherts signed a three-year contract with [[Castleford Tigers]] to start from the 2020 season.<ref name="BBC2Jul19">{{cite web |title=Derrell Olpherts: Castleford sign Salford Red Devils winger on three-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/48845559 |website=BBC Sport |accessdate=3 July 2019 |date=2 July 2019}}</ref><ref name="YEP2Jul19">{{cite web |last1=Smith |first1=Peter |title=Flying Salford Red Devils winger Derrell Olpherts heading to Castleford Tigers for 2020 |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/flying-salford-red-devils-winger-derrell-olpherts-heading-to-castleford-tigers-for-2020-1-9853865 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |accessdate=3 July 2019 |date=2 July 2019 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20190703065344/https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/flying-salford-red-devils-winger-derrell-olpherts-heading-to-castleford-tigers-for-2020-1-9853865 | archivedate=3 July 2019}}</ref> Head coach [[Daryl Powell]] said of him: "Derrell has been a standout winger in Super League this season. His ability to break tackles and make the game quick is outstanding."<ref name="BBC2Jul19" />

Olpherts made his Castleford début on 2 February 2020 against the [[Toronto Wolfpack]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Hafez |first=Shamoon |date=2 February 2020 |title=Super League: Toronto Wolfpack 10-28 Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/51266825 |access-date=23 July 2022 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He scored his first try for the club on 7 February against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web |date=7 February 2020 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 16-12 Wigan Warriors - Olpherts sets up win with first Tigers try |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/51359805 |access-date=23 July 2022 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He finished the [[2020 Castleford Tigers season|2020 season]] as Castleford's joint-highest tryscorer alongside [[Greg Eden]].

On 17 July 2021, Olpherts played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|website=BBC Sport|date=17 July 2021|access-date=18 July 2021}}</ref>

In round 20 of the [[Super League XXVII|2022 Super League season]], Olpherts scored four tries and was named Man of the Match against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], marking his first [[hat-trick]] for Castleford.<ref>{{cite web |date=22 July 2022 |title=Super League: Derrell Olpherts stars as ruthless Castleford Tigers stun Hull FC 46-18 |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12657086/super-league-derrell-olpherts-stars-as-ruthless-castleford-tigers-stun-hull-fc-46-18 |access-date=23 July 2022 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |date=22 July 2022 |title=Super League: Hull FC 18-46 Castleford - Lee Radford's Tigers come from behind to win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62238358 |access-date=23 July 2022 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

On 26 August 2022, Castleford confirmed that Olpherts would leave the club at the end of the season upon the expiry of his contract.<ref>{{cite web |date=26 August 2022 |title=Tigers departing club at end of 2022 |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8260 |access-date=11 October 2022 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=26 August 2022 |title=Castleford confirm six departures at the end of the season |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-six-players-leaving/ |access-date=11 October 2022 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

=== Leeds Rhinos===
On 20 October 2022, Olpherts signed a two-year contract with the [[Leeds Rhinos]] to start from the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Derrell Olpherts: Leeds Rhinos sign Castleford Tigers outside back on two-year deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63327447 |website=BBC Sport |accessdate=22 October 2022|date=20 October 2022}}</ref>
Olpherts played 13 games for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-2023-season-review-picking-out-the-best-and-worst-players-performances-results-4355920/|title=Leeds Rhinos' 2023 season review: picking out the best and worst players, performances, results|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 12 March 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=12 Mar 2024|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Out of favour Leeds Rhinos winger Derrell Olpherts makes permanent Championship move|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/out-of-favour-leeds-rhinos-winger-derrell-olpherts-makes-permanent-championship-move}}</ref>
In his first season at the club, he scored 27 tries in 28 matches. Wakefield would win three trophies in 2024, the 1895 cup, the league leaders shield and the RFL Championship defeating Toulouse Olympique in the grand final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cqjr5lvxk4lo|title=Jowitt breaks points record as Wakefield cruise to Grand Final win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 16 November 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=16 Nov 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Derrell Olpherts seals fresh Championship transfer move after Wakefield Trinity departure|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/featherstone-rovers-sign-former-wakefield-trinity-winger-derrell-olpherts}}</ref>

===Batley Bulldogs===
On 3 February 2026, it was reported that he had signed for [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]] until the end of the 2026 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=3 Feb 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title= Ex-Super League stalwart lands new Championship deal following Featherstone Rovers’ demise |url= https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/derrell-olpherts-batley-bulldogs-for-2026}}</ref>
